texto: Happiness is what you choose, what you follow, not what follows you. These are the things I have seen, these are the things I now know, these are the things I will carry with me as I go.
explicacao: Citação de Allison Winn Scotch sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Happiness is what you choose, what you follow, not what follows you. These are the things I have …” articula reflexao, happiness, these em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: Happiness is what you choose, what you follow, not what follows you. These are the things I have seen, these are the things I now know, these are the things I will carry with me as I go.

texto: Outside, with Labor Day having come and gone, summer is fighting a dying battle against the fall air. The leaves are hanging perilously on the trees, knowing full well they're going to make the plunge, clinging on as if they stand a chance not to. The garbage smell that has wafted around us for the better part of August is dissipating, ushered out with the humidity, and in its place a briskness is filtering in, like something you'd smell from a bottle of Tide.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Allison Winn Scotch: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Outside, with Labor Day having come and gone, summer is fighting a dying battle against the fall …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, autumn, fall pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de fall.

The garbage smell that has wafted around us for the better part of August is dissipating, ushered out with the humidity, and in its place a briskness is filtering in, like something you'd smell from a bottle of Tide.

texto: ...I realize that lessons are meant to be learned, honored even, or else you can spend your life running so far from them that you erect a false existence around the very thing you should be embracing.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Allison Winn Scotch sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “...I realize that lessons are meant to be learned, honored even, or else you can spend your life …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, life-lessons, realize, útil em momentos de termino e reflexao.
